content: Hey, I'm training for a triathlon next month, and I'm wondering if it's a good idea to get a dog to be my running buddy.
That's a great idea! Having a dog can be a wonderful motivator. What kind of breed are you thinking of getting? Something with high energy to keep up with your training?
I'm not sure yet, but I've always been a fan of bigger dogs. I've got a big backyard in Tucson, Arizona, where I live, so space isn't an issue.

Tucson is a beautiful city. With your experience as a truck driver, I'm sure you're used to being on the road, but having a dog will definitely keep you more grounded at home. Have you considered how you'll balance your training schedule with caring for a new pet?
Yeah, I've thought about it. My daughter, Sophia, has offered to help out when I'm away on long hauls. She's actually the one who's been bugging me to get a dog in the first place!
It's great that you've got a support system in place. What do you like to do in your free time, aside from training for the triathlon? Any hobbies that you're passionate about?
I'm really into speedcubing, and I've also recently taken up beekeeping. There's something about working with the bees that's really calming.
That's fascinating! I've never met anyone who's into beekeeping before. Do you find that it helps you relax after a long drive?
Definitely. And I've also been enjoying gardening in my backyard. There's something therapeutic about watching everything grow.
Gardening can be very fulfilling. Have you thought about what you'll name your new dog, once you get one?
Actually, Sophia and I were thinking of naming it 'Mocha' if we get a brown dog. We'll see what happens, though!
I love that name! Mocha is a great choice for a dog. You should also consider getting a dog that's comfortable with your love of Japanese cuisine - maybe one that won't steal your sushi!
Ha! Yeah, that's a good point. I'll have to keep an eye on my food when I'm cooking dinner. Thanks for the advice, I think I'm one step closer to getting my new running buddy!
You're welcome, Rowan! It was great chatting with you. Good luck with your triathlon training, and I hope you find the perfect furry companion soon!
Thanks, I'll need all the luck I can get! The triathlon is on June 15th, so I've got about a month to get everything ready.
You've got this, Rowan! Just remember to stay focused, and don't hesitate to reach out if you need any more advice or recommendations.
